headline_risk_context.headline: Markets rally after U.S.-Iran temporary ceasefire; oil prices plunge, prompting broad regional risk-on flows
notes: Major wire services reported a temporary U.S.-Iran ceasefire announcement that materially reduced oil risk premium and triggered global equity rallies on 2026-04-08.
This is supporting external context for Taiwan market sentiment; it is not a structured market feed.

market_driver_summary.primary_driver: U.S.-Iran temporary ceasefire & sharp fall in oil prices (risk-on impulse)
secondary_drivers: US monetary policy / Treasury yields uncertainty
likely_affected_groups: Cyclical sectors and ETF/indices sensitive to foreign flows
market_driver_summary.why_it_matters: A ceasefire that materially lowers oil prices reduces near-term input-cost risk for Asian economies and removes a key geopolitical risk premium — this tends to unlock foreign risk appetite, contributing to large foreign net-buying and broad index gains (as seen on 2026-04-08). That flow dynamic is particularly important for Taiwan where foreign flows and semiconductor exports drive index moves.
market_driver_summary.what_would_invalidate: Ceasefire collapses or is revealed as temporary with immediate resumption of hostilities (oil spikes); surprise hawkish Fed communication driving yields sharply higher; re-escalation of trade/tariff actions targeting Taiwan supply chains; or major negative earnings surprises from key semiconductor firms.

persistent_watch: US monetary policy path & Treasury yields (affects FX, capital flows)
Geopolitical developments in Middle East (oil & shipping risk)
Large-cap semiconductor / AI supply-chain earnings and guidance
Trade / tariff policy headlines that could re-introduce structural risk

current_high_priority.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire & oil price move
current_high_priority.why: Immediate driver for 04-08 risk-on rally; directly tied to oil price drop and cross-regional equity gains
current_high_priority.expected_actionability: High — drives index-level risk appetite and foreign net flows

deprioritized.title: April tariff shock (early April)
deprioritized.why: Still relevant historically, but the immediate market reaction has shifted toward geopolitics/oil; downgrade until new tariff headlines re-emerge

surprise_buckets: Ceasefire breakdown → oil spike → risk-off reversal
Unexpected hawkish Fed signaling → yield spike & equity rotation
Major semiconductor earnings miss or supply-chain disruption
